title: chroma-reasoner object-centric colour plan
description: The intermediate representation between the LLM reasoner and the colorizer. Object-centric: each region anchors a colour, contextual modifiers deform its prior, and the resolved colour plus rationale make the plan inspectable and editable before pixels are committed.

plan_version.description: Schema version. Bump only with a migration note in docs/phase1.md.

image_id.description: Identifier of the source image (e.g. COCO stem '000000000139'). Optional; plans can be authored before an image is chosen.

prompt.description: The user's abstract context prompt this plan realizes (e.g. 'melancholic 1910s seaside'). Empty/absent for purely automatic plans.

global.description: Image-wide effects that do not route through a single object: film-stock/fading emulation, global saturation caps, colour temperature. Applied after per-region colours.

id.description: Stable identifier for editing/diffing plans. Optional.

object.description: Canonical object class name; the lookup key into the knowledge base (e.g. 'dress', 'foliage', 'car').

grounding_phrase.description: Natural-language phrase for Grounding DINO to localize the region (e.g. "the woman's long dress").

base_prior.description: Reference/id of the KB colour-distribution prior this colour was resolved from. Null until the KB exists (Phase 3) or when the reasoner falls back to its own prior.

modifiers.description: Contextual factors the reasoner selected as operative for this region, in application order (composition is order-sensitive).

tolerance_delta_e.description: Acceptable CIE76 ΔE radius around resolved_colour for palette-adherence evaluation. Wide for weakly-constrained objects (a car could be many blues), tight for strongly-constrained ones (grass). Default 10 if absent.

confidence.description: Reasoner's confidence that this colour assignment is right, given content constraints and prior strength.

rationale.description: Human-readable justification: which priors and modifiers produced this colour. The auditability of the whole system lives here.

family.description: Modifier family. Open vocabulary by design (the factor list is unbounded); established families: era, mood, season, geography, weather, time_of_day, occasion, condition, socioeconomic, formality.

effect.description: How this modifier deforms the colour distribution, stated qualitatively (e.g. 'muted dyes, low saturation', 'orange/brown shift').

lab_colour.description: CIE L*a*b* (D65). L in [0,100]; a,b in [-128,127]. Lab is the working space because ΔE and colorization math live there; convert to hex/RGB only for display.
